navigation_overview:
Mifos is a comprehensive microfinance and core banking platform built on Apache Fineract. The application uses a Material Design layout with a persistent top navigation bar and a left icon-based sidebar. The top navigation bar displays the Mifos logo on the left and menu items for Institution, Accounting, Reports, Admin, Self Service, and Configuration Wizard, along with icons for global search, language selection, notifications, and user profile on the right. The Institution menu contains links to Clients, Groups, Centers, Accounting, Reports, Admin, and Self Service. The left sidebar contains icon-based shortcuts to commonly used sections including Clients, Groups, Centers, and Products; each icon navigates to the corresponding page in the main content area. Breadcrumbs appear at the top of the content area showing the navigation path. Entity listing pages display data in Material Design tables with search, filter, sort, and pagination controls. Detail pages display entity information in card layouts with action buttons for state transitions. Forms use Material Design input fields with inline validation.

---

module_summaries.summary:
Users can sign in by selecting language and tenant, entering username and password, toggle 'Remember me', and use 'Forgot Password' to recover credentials or submit to authenticate.
Displays branding and authentication UI: language selector, Tenant dropdown, Username and Password fields, Remember me checkbox, Login button (enabled when valid) and Forgot Password link; shows validation/errors.

Users can view the home welcome screen, search recent system activity, and navigate to the Dashboard.
Displays a welcome card ('Welcome, mifos!'), a Search Activity input, a Dashboard button, and system version information for Mifos and Fineract.

Users can search activities and view office-level client trends and financial summary metrics on the Dashboard.
Displays a Search Activity field, a Client Trends chart with New Clients and Closed Clients legends, and summary cards for Amount Pending/Disbursed and Amount Collected (shows 'No Data' if empty).

Users can invoke a global search from the top toolbar to find and navigate to Clients, Groups, Loans, and Savings accounts.
Displays a search input and live dropdown of grouped matching results showing entity name, identifier and status, or a 'No results found' message when there are no matches.

Users can search, filter, import, and create clients and open client detail pages via the client list.
Displays a clients data table with Name (clickable link), Account No., External ID, colored Status chips (Pending/Active/Closed/Rejected/Withdrawn), Office, Staff, search field and status filter bar, plus Import Client and Create Client actions.

Users can search, import, or create groups and associate existing clients when creating a group.
Displays a groups table with Group Name (clickable link), Account Number, Office, colored Status chips, Staff, and a Create Group form (Name, Office, Staff, Submitted On, Active checkbox, External Id and Add Clients interface).

Users can import or create centers and select/add groups to a center record.
Displays a centers table with Name (clickable link), Account #, External Id, Status and Office Name, plus a Create Center form (Name, Office, Staff, Active checkbox, Submitted On and Select/Add Groups).

Users can filter and browse loan products and create a new loan product using a 6-step stepper wizard.
Displays a filter bar and data table of loan products with Name (clickable link), Short Name, Expiry Date and Status, and the create wizard with Step 1 (Details) fields such as Product Name, Short Name, Fund, dates and product options.

Users can filter and view savings products and create new savings products via a stepper wizard.
Displays a filter bar and table with Name (clickable link) and Short Name, and a create wizard with steps including Details (Product Name, Short Name, Description, External Id), Currency and Terms fields.

Users can view share products and create new share products using a multi-step wizard.
Displays a share products table with Product Name (clickable link), Short Name and Total Shares, and a create wizard (7 steps) with Details (Product Name, Short Name, Description), Currency and Terms including total number of shares and pricing.

Users can view, create and manage charges applicable to loans, savings, clients or shares.
Displays a charges table with Name (clickable link), Charge Applies To, Is Penalty, Is Active and Is Paid Derived, and a create form with Charge Name, Charge Applies To, Currency and Charge Time Type options specific to entity.

Users can view and create floating rate definitions, mark a base lending rate, and configure rate periods.
Displays a floating rates table with Floating Rate Name (clickable link), Is Base Lending Rate, Is Active and Created By, and a create form with name, base/active checkboxes and a Rate Periods table (From Date, Interest Rate, Is Differential Rate).

Users can create and manage delinquency ranges and buckets to classify overdue loans and collections behavior.
Displays delinquency ranges table with Classification (clickable link), Minimum Age Days and Maximum Age Days, a delinquency buckets table, and forms to create ranges (Classification, Minimum/Maximum Age Days) and buckets.

Users can initiate and progress a multi-step loan application from a client's detail page and submit it for processing.
Displays the loan application wizard with fields like Product Name (select to auto-populate defaults), Loan Officer, Loan Purpose, Fund, Submitted On, Expected Disbursement Date, Principal (product-bounded), Number of Repayments, frequency, Interest Rate and External ID.

Users can create a savings account for a client by selecting a product and configuring interest, posting rules and charges.
Displays a creation form with Product Name (auto-populates product defaults), Field Officer, Submitted On, Nominal Annual Interest Rate, Interest Compounding/Posting/Calculation periods, Days in Year, Minimum Opening Balance, Lock-in Period, Allow Overdraft option and product charges list.

Users can apply for a share account from a client page by selecting a share product, choosing requested shares and specifying charge payment details, then submit the application.
Displays a share account application form with Share Product (auto-populate), Submitted On, Requested Shares (product-bounded), Application Date, Savings Account for Charges dropdown, External ID, Charges section and the resulting account status (Submitted and Pending Approval).

Users can create fixed or recurring deposit accounts and perform lifecycle actions such as approve, activate or prematurely close the account.
Displays a creation form with Fixed Deposit Product, Deposit Amount, Deposit Period and unit, Maturity Instructions, and an account detail view showing deposit amount, maturity date and amount, interest rate, status and action buttons (Approve, Activate, Premature Close).

Users can browse the hierarchical chart of GL accounts, create new GL accounts and set account properties and parent relationships.
Displays a hierarchical GL tree by account type with GL Code, Account Name, color-coded Account Type, Manual Entries Allowed indicator, Usage (Header or Detail), Description, Tag and Parent Account, plus a Create GL Account action.

Users can view, filter and add journal entries, and search entries by office, account, date range, transaction ID and type.
Displays a journal entries table with Entry ID, Office, Transaction Date, Created Date, Transaction ID, Type (Debit/Credit), GL Account and Amount, and a create entry form with Office, Currency, Transaction Date, payment details and entry lines.

Users can create, edit and view accounting rules that map business activities to debit and credit GL accounts across offices.
Displays a rules table with Rule Name (clickable link), Office, Debit Account(s) and Credit Account(s), and a create/edit form with Office, Rule Name, Debit/Credit account or tag selection and multi-entry allowances.

Users can create and manage provisioning criteria with definitions that determine provisioning percentages by loan product and aging category.
Displays a criteria table with Criteria Name and Created Date, and a create form containing Definitions rows specifying Loan Product, Category (STANDARD, SUB-STANDARD, DOUBTFUL, LOSS), Minimum/Maximum Age, Provisioning Percentage and associated GL accounts.

Users can view the organizational office hierarchy and create new offices with parent relationships and opening dates.
Displays a hierarchical offices table with Office Name (clickable link), External ID, Parent Office, Opening Date and hierarchy position, and an Office detail view with edit option.

Users can create, view and manage employee records and mark staff as loan officers for assignment in workflows.
Displays an employees table with Name (clickable link), Office, Is Loan Officer indicator and Status, and a create form with Office, First and Last Name, Is Loan Officer checkbox, Mobile Number, Joining Date, Is Active and External ID.

Users can create and manage tellers and assign cashiers with start/end dates and activation status.
Displays a tellers table with Teller Name (clickable link), Office, Status and Description, a Create Teller form (Office, Teller Name, Start/End Date, Status) and a Teller detail page showing teller info, edit option and Cashiers assignments list.

Users can create and manage application user accounts, link users to staff, and enforce password and email validations.
Displays a users table with Username (clickable link), First Name, Last Name, Email, Office and Status, and a create form with Username, First/Last Name, Email, Office, Staff dropdown and password/confirmation fields with validation.

Users can browse reports by category, supply parameters, run reports and export or view report outputs.
Displays a reports list grouped by category with Name (clickable link) and Type, parameter forms (Office, Branch, Currency, Product, Date Range, Loan Officer, Fund, etc.) and report output as a sortable, paginated table with export options.

Users can execute single account transfers by specifying source and destination accounts and create/manage standing instructions for recurring transfers.
Displays a transfer form with From/To Office, Client, Account Type and Account, Transfer Amount, Transfer Date and Description (with balance validation/error), and a standing instructions table listing existing instructions.

Users can create and manage tax components and groups, mapping tax percentages to debit/credit GL accounts and effective dates.
Displays a tax components table with Name (clickable link), Percentage, Debit Account Type and Account, Credit Account Type and Account, Start Date, and a create form for tax component details.

Users can manage organization-level settings such as holidays and their rescheduling rules for affected offices and schedules.
Displays a Holidays table with Name, From/To Dates, Status and Rescheduling Type, and a create holiday form with Name, From/To Dates, Rescheduling Type, Repayments Rescheduled To date, Description and applicable Offices.

Users can view, enable/disable and configure scheduler batch jobs and inspect their run history and next run times.
Displays a scheduler jobs table with Job Name, Is Active toggle, CRON Expression, Previous Run Status with times, Next Run Time, Currently Running indicator and action buttons for each job.

Users can open the profile menu and log out to terminate their authenticated session and clear credentials.
Displays a profile dropdown with Profile Settings and Log Out; logging out clears the auth token and redirects to the login page, preventing access to authenticated pages.

test_cases.title: Login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Tenant selection if applicable, Username, Password)
Click "Login"
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Dashboard after successful authentication.

test_cases.title: Login button enables only after required fields are filled
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave required fields empty and observe the "Login" button state
Fill all required fields (Username, Password)
Observe the "Login" button becomes enabled
test_cases.expected_result: Login button becomes enabled only after required fields are filled.

test_cases.title: Direct URL access while logged out redirects to login
test_cases.preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: Open a new browser or incognito window to ensure no active session.
Enter the URL for the Dashboard in the address bar and press Enter.
Observe the page navigation.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to the Login page instead of the protected page.

test_cases.title: Tenant dropdown defaults to default
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Observe the Tenant dropdown value in the authentication interface
test_cases.expected_result: Tenant dropdown is set to "default" by default.

test_cases.title: Forgot Password? link appears below the Login button

steps: Observe the presence and position of the "Forgot Password?" link relative to the "Login" button
test_cases.expected_result: "Forgot Password?" link is visible below the Login button.

test_cases.title: Page refresh while logged in keeps user logged in
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in on an authenticated page
steps: While on Dashboard, verify authenticated UI is visible (e.g., top nav with user profile).
Refresh the browser using the reload control or F5.
Verify the page re-renders and you remain on the authenticated page without being redirected to login.
test_cases.expected_result: After refresh the user remains authenticated and the page reloads without redirecting to the Login page.

test_cases.title: Already-logged-in user navigating to login URL is redirected to authenticated landing page
test_cases.preconditions: User is already logged in
steps: While signed in, enter the Login page URL in the address bar and press Enter.
Observe the resulting navigation.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected away from the Login page to the authenticated landing page (Home Page) instead of seeing the login form.

test_cases.title: Log Out terminates authenticated session and redirects to login
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on any authenticated page.
steps: Click the user profile icon in the top-right corner
Click "Log Out" in the profile dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Authenticated session is terminated and the user is redirected to the login page.

test_cases.title: Logout from user menu terminates session and shows login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in
steps: From any authenticated page, open the user/profile menu in the top navigation.
Click the Logout option.
Observe the page after logout.
test_cases.expected_result: The session is terminated and the Login page is displayed with user-specific UI removed.

test_cases.title: Protected routes inaccessible after logout
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out after an active session
steps: After logging out, attempt to navigate to Client Management by using the left sidebar or entering its URL.
Observe the navigation and page content.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is prevented from accessing the protected route and is redirected to the Login page or shown access denied.

test_cases.title: Browser refresh after logout does not restore authenticated session
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out
steps: Immediately after logging out, refresh the browser using the reload control or F5.
Check the page content after the refresh.
test_cases.expected_result: Refreshing the browser does not restore an authenticated session; the user remains logged out and no protected content reappears.

test_cases.title: Using browser back after logout does not restore authenticated page
test_cases.preconditions: User has logged out and the login page is displayed.
steps: Click the browser Back button to attempt to return to a previously viewed authenticated page
Observe the displayed page
test_cases.expected_result: The application displays the login page rather than the authenticated content.
test_cases.spec_evidence: After logout, any attempt to navigate to an authenticated page redirects to the login page.

test_cases.title: Log Out clears authentication token from browser storage
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on any authenticated page.
steps: Click the user profile icon in the top-right corner
Click "Log Out" in the profile dropdown
Inspect browser authentication storage (cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage)
test_cases.expected_result: No authentication token is present in browser storage after logout.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Clicking "Log Out" clears the authentication token.

test_cases.title: Reveal profile dropdown from user profile icon
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on any authenticated page.
steps: Click the user profile icon in the top-right corner
test_cases.expected_result: A dropdown is displayed listing Profile Settings and Log Out.
